assembly_instructions: Read the GLM-4.5 stack as a TALL, NARROW factory: 96 transformer blocks climb the violet residual pillar at X=0 (8 are rendered, the rest are implied by the etched gridline). Every block has GQA attention (96 query heads sharing 8 KV heads) on the left half and a fine-grained MoE FFN on the right (1 shared + 160 routed experts, top-8) — both are exploded into sub-components in the side panels. The amber column at X=-3 is the GQA KV cache. The orange ring at X=6 is the routed-expert bank with its sigmoid router and group-limited dispatch (a token is allowed to land on experts in at most 4 of 8 device groups — this is what makes EP all-to-all manageable). The bronze cube at X=2.5/Y=8 is the MTP head used for speculative decoding. The two slabs in the upper-right are the dual MODE selectors (think / no-think) that share the same weights. The far-right translucent slab marks the parallelism plan. Click any part for FLOPs, memory, parallelism class, scratch-build pseudocode, the dominant bottleneck, and improvement ideas.

info.summary: 355B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts language model from Zhipu AI / Z.ai (32B activated per token). Deep-and-narrow design: 96 layers × 5120 hidden dim. GQA attention (96 Q heads / 8 KV heads, partial RoPE, QK-Norm), 160 routed + 1 shared expert per layer with sigmoid loss-free routing and group-limited top-8 dispatch, MTP head for speculative decoding, and a hybrid thinking / non-thinking mode that shares weights. Pre-trained on ~23 T tokens, post-trained for agentic + reasoning + coding with self-distillation RL.
info.description:
GLM-4.5 is Z.ai's flagship open MoE for the 'ARC' (Agentic, Reasoning, Coding) trinity. Compared with GLM-4 it is much sparser (only 32B of 355B activated per token), DEEPER (96 layers vs 40) but NARROWER (5120 hidden dim vs 6144), and adds first-class hybrid reasoning: a single set of weights answers either in 'think' or 'no-think' mode controlled by a system prompt. Routing uses a sigmoid gate with the auxiliary-loss-free bias trick (DeepSeek-V3 style) and an extra group-limit constraint (each token may activate experts on AT MOST 4 of 8 device groups), which keeps cross-node all-to-all bounded. QK-Norm + partial RoPE replace V3's full-RoPE on attention. A single Multi-Token Prediction head provides ~1.8× speculative decoding speedup. Training is 3-stage (general → reasoning → long-context) on ~23T tokens, with FP8 GEMMs and BF16 master weights.

Design rationale, in one line each:
• Deep-narrow: more layers ≫ wider FFN per FLOP for reasoning tasks at fixed compute.
• Group-limited routing: caps EP all-to-all bandwidth, the real bottleneck on H800-class clusters.
• Hybrid think mode: avoids maintaining two separate models (chat + reasoning).
• MTP head: free speculative decoding without a draft model.

facts.label: QK norm
facts.value: RMSNorm on Q and K (replaces soft-cap)

comparisons.delta: GLM-4 was a dense decoder (~32B at the upper end). GLM-4.5 jumps to a 355B sparse MoE with 32B activated — same per-token FLOPs but ~11× more capacity. Adds GQA (V4 was MHA), QK-Norm, partial RoPE, MTP, hybrid thinking, and the sigmoid loss-free balance trick. Vocabulary stays in the 150K range.

Two generations of pre-norm dense decoders with full RoPE and bilinear FFN. GLM-4.5 keeps RoPE (partial now) but otherwise replaces every load-bearing block: SwiGLU FFN → MoE with shared expert, MHA → GQA, plain attention → QK-Norm + partial-RoPE, SFT-only post-training → multi-stage RL with self-distillation. Goes from 32K → 128K context.

Same family of ideas (MoE + shared expert + auxiliary-loss-free balancing + MTP) but tuned differently: GLM-4.5 is SMALLER (355B vs 671B), DEEPER (96 vs 61 layers), and uses GQA + partial RoPE + QK-Norm where V3 uses MLA. V3 has 256 experts top-8 with no group constraint; GLM-4.5 has 160 experts top-8 with group-limited routing (≤4 of 8 groups). V3 supports 128K via YaRN; GLM-4.5 also targets 128K. V3 trained in FP8 mixed; GLM-4.5 also uses FP8 GEMMs.

Versus Qwen3-235B-A22B: GLM-4.5 has more total params (355B vs 235B) and more activated (32B vs 22B), more layers (96 vs 94), but fewer experts (160 vs 128, both top-8). Both have hybrid thinking modes. Versus public GPT-4 estimates: GLM-4.5 is much sparser per token (32B activated vs estimated ~280B+ in GPT-4 dense or ~110B in rumoured GPT-4-MoE), uses GQA at extreme ratios (12:1) for cheaper KV, and is open-weight.

parts.name: Input Tokens (BPE)
parts.role: Token-ID stream (vocab=151,552). Pre-training corpora packed with sample-aware attention masking. Tokeniser is the GLM tiktoken-style BPE inherited from GLM-4.
parts.algorithm:
tokens = bpe.encode(text)
batch  = pack(tokens, max_len=32_768)
mask   = sample_attention_mask(batch)
parts.bottleneck: Loader-side BPE throughput on the CPU.

parts.name: Embedding (V=151K, d=5120)
parts.role: Token-embedding lookup, tied with the LM head. ~776M params (BF16, ~1.55 GiB).
parts.algorithm: h0 = E[token_ids]   # gather, sharded on V across TP ranks

parts.name: Residual Stream (d=5120, 96 layers)
parts.role: Pre-norm residual stream carrying activations through all 96 blocks. Each block writes y = x + sublayer(RMSNorm(x)).
parts.memory_footprint: B·T·d × dtype per layer of activation
parts.algorithm:
x = x + attn(rmsnorm_pre(x))
x = x + ffn(rmsnorm_post(x))

parts.name: × 96 Transformer Layers (deep & narrow)
parts.role: Marker for the 96-layer depth — only 8 are rendered. Deep-narrow shape (96×5120) was chosen over wide-shallow (e.g. 48×8192) for reasoning quality at fixed FLOPs.
parts.bottleneck: PP bubble — 96 layers across PP=8 stages cost ~7 microbatches of bubble unless DualPipe / 1F1B is used.

parts.name: Block 1
parts.role: First Transformer block. Pre-norm GQA attention + MoE FFN, mHC-free (plain residual).

parts.name: Block 2
parts.role: Block 2. Identical layer recipe to block_01.

parts.name: Block 3
parts.role: Block 3. Exploded into sub-components on the right (X=4) and left (X=-1.6).

parts.name: Block 4
parts.role: Block 4.

parts.name: Block 5
parts.role: Block 5.

parts.name: Block 6
parts.role: Block 6.

parts.name: Block 95
parts.role: Block 95. (Layers 7–94 elided.)

parts.name: Block 96 (final)
parts.role: Final block before the prediction head.

parts.name: Attention · Pre-RMSNorm (γ)
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm with a learned per-channel γ ∈ R^d. Trained by AdamW alongside everything else.
parts.algorithm:
x_hat = x / sqrt(mean(x*x) + eps)
y     = γ * x_hat

parts.name: GQA · QKV Projection (96 Q, 8 KV)
parts.role: Single fused QKV GEMM: Q is projected to 96 × 128-dim heads; K and V to only 8 × 128-dim heads (12-way KV sharing). This is the key to GLM-4.5's small KV footprint.
parts.memory_footprint: W_QKV: d × (n_h + 2·n_kv) × d_h ≈ 64 M params (BF16, ~128 MB)
parts.algorithm:
Q = x @ W_Q  # [B,T, 96, 128]
K = x @ W_K  # [B,T, 8,  128]
V = x @ W_V  # [B,T, 8,  128]
Q, K = qk_rmsnorm(Q, K)        # QK-Norm
Q, K = apply_partial_rope(Q, K) # half dims rotated
parts.bottleneck: Compute-bound at training; bandwidth-bound at decode (small batch).

parts.name: QK-Norm (RMSNorm on Q & K)
parts.role: Per-head RMSNorm on Q and K BEFORE the attention dot-product. Replaces V3-style logit soft-cap. Stabilises FP8 GEMMs and removes a class of attention spikes.
parts.algorithm:
Q = γ_q * Q / sqrt(mean(Q*Q, dim=-1) + eps)
K = γ_k * K / sqrt(mean(K*K, dim=-1) + eps)

parts.name: Partial RoPE (half-dim rotated)
parts.role: Rotary position encoding applied to ONLY half of each head's channels; the other half stays positional-free. Cheaper than full RoPE and empirically equally good after QK-Norm.
parts.algorithm:
rope_dims = 64 of 128
Q[..., :rope_dims], K[..., :rope_dims] = rotate(... , pos)

parts.name: FlashAttention-2 (GQA dense)
parts.role: Dense FlashAttention over the full causal context (no sparsity at GLM-4.5; sparsity is reserved for the inference engine via paged KV).
parts.algorithm:
logits = (Q @ K.T) / sqrt(d_h)   # tile-streamed
attn   = softmax(logits)
out    = attn @ V
parts.bottleneck: O(T²) at long context — RING attention or local-global splits would help but GLM-4.5 stays dense.

parts.name: Attention · O-Projection
parts.role: Output projection back to d=5120. Adds into the residual stream.
parts.memory_footprint: n_h·d_h × d ≈ 63 M params (~126 MB BF16)
parts.algorithm:
y = out_heads.flatten(-2) @ W_O
residual += y

parts.name: GQA KV Cache (8 KV heads)
parts.role: Per-layer paged KV cache. With only 8 KV heads (GQA group ratio 12), the cache is ~12× smaller than full MHA at the same context.
parts.memory_footprint: ≈ 2 × 8 × 128 × FP8 = 2 KB / token / layer; 96 layers × 128K ctx ≈ 25 GiB / sequence
parts.algorithm:
cache = PagedKV(stride=1, dtype=FP8)
# write on prefill, read on decode
# evict-cold-prefix policy (e.g., RadixAttention)
parts.bottleneck: HBM capacity sets max batchable context per GPU.
improvement_ideas: Quantise KV to INT4 with per-channel scales for ~2× more context.

parts.name: FFN · Post-Attn RMSNorm
parts.role: Pre-norm RMSNorm before the MoE FFN sublayer.
parts.algorithm: x_hat = x / sqrt(mean(x*x) + eps); y = γ * x_hat

parts.name: Top-8 Sigmoid Router
parts.role: Per-token affinity = sigmoid(x @ W_r) + b_balance. Top-8 of 160 routed experts. Auxiliary-loss-FREE: b_balance is updated by a tiny rule that nudges over-loaded experts down.
parts.algorithm:
scores = sigmoid(x @ W_router) + b_balance
topk_idx, topk_w = topk(scores, 8)
topk_w = topk_w / topk_w.sum()  # renormalise
# b_balance updated each step:
# b -= lr_b * (load_e - mean_load)
parts.bottleneck: Sequential with the all-to-all dispatch that follows.
improvement_ideas: Speculative dispatch using a 1-step-stale router prediction.
Fuse with the FFN-pre-norm above (norm + router in one kernel).

parts.name: Group-Limited Dispatch (≤4 of 8 device groups)
parts.role: Constraint imposed on the top-8 selection: a single token may activate experts on at most 4 of 8 EP device groups. Bounds cross-node all-to-all traffic.
improvement_ideas: Dynamic group budget — relax to 6 groups under low load, tighten to 3 under contention.

parts.name: Expert All-to-All (dispatch + combine)
parts.role: Two NCCL all-to-all collectives per layer: dispatch tokens to chosen experts, then combine outputs back. The group-limit above keeps this bounded.
parts.algorithm:
grouped = bucket_by_expert(x, topk_idx)
recv    = nccl.all_to_all(grouped)
# expert FFN ↓
out_grp = bucket_by_origin(expert_out)
out     = nccl.all_to_all(out_grp)
improvement_ideas: Overlap dispatch with the previous attention O-proj.
Token grouping by destination rank to coalesce sends.

parts.name: Routed Experts (160, top-8)
parts.role: Per-MoE-layer bank of 160 fine-grained SwiGLU experts (intermediate dim 1408). 8 active per token. FP8 GEMM with BF16 master weights.
parts.memory_footprint: 160 × 3·d·d_ff × BF16 ≈ 11 GiB / layer of routed weights; 96 layers ≈ 1.05 TiB
improvement_ideas: Group GEMM (next part) to fuse 8 expert calls.
FP4 QAT for further memory wins (DeepSeek-V4-style).

parts.name: Shared Expert (×1, always-on)
parts.role: One always-on expert that complements the 8 routed ones. Carries 'common' computation and stabilises routing in early training.

parts.name: Group GEMM (fused expert FFN)
parts.role: cuBLASLt grouped-GEMM kernel that runs all 8 (capacity-padded) experts in a single launch.
parts.algorithm:
# per group g in this layer:
Y_g = X_g @ W_gate[g]
H_g = silu(Y_g) * (X_g @ W_up[g])
O_g = H_g @ W_down[g]
# all groups in one launch, padded to capacity

parts.name: FP8 GEMM (E4M3 forward / E5M2 backward)
parts.role: Mixed-precision marker: forward GEMMs in E4M3, backward in E5M2 (Hopper fp8). BF16 master weights kept for AdamW updates.
parts.memory_footprint: FP8 weights ~2× smaller than BF16 in HBM during forward.
parts.algorithm:
W_fp8 = quantize(W_master, scale=group_per_block)
activations are dynamically scaled at GEMM boundaries

parts.name: LM Head (tied with E)
parts.role: Projection back to 151K vocab; tied with the embedding table. Trained by AdamW.
parts.memory_footprint: Same tensor as the embedding table.
parts.algorithm:
logits = h_final @ E.T
p      = softmax(logits / T)
improvement_ideas: Speculative decoding via the MTP head.
Top-k prune before softmax for cheap sampling.

parts.name: MTP Head (depth=1, speculative decoding)
parts.role: Single Multi-Token-Prediction head trained jointly to predict t+1 alongside the main t head. At inference enables ~1.8× speculative decoding without a draft model.
parts.flops_per_token: ≈ one extra block + LM head GEMM
parts.algorithm:
h_aux  = mtp_block(h_final)
logits_aux = h_aux @ E.T
loss_aux   = CE(logits_aux, target_t+2)

parts.name: LM Loss (cross-entropy)
parts.role: Primary next-token cross-entropy loss.

parts.name: MTP Loss (small weight)
parts.role: Auxiliary loss on the MTP head.

parts.name: Mode: 'think' (CoT enabled)
parts.role: System-prompt-switched reasoning mode. Same weights as no-think; different system prompt + decoding budget. Selected automatically by classifier or explicitly.

parts.name: Mode: 'no-think' (direct answer)
parts.role: Fast direct-answer mode. Uses the same weights but truncates CoT.

parts.name: AdamW (β1=0.9, β2=0.95)
parts.role: Single optimiser for the entire model. Adam state in FP32 with BF16 master weights. ZeRO-1 sharding across DP.
parts.memory_footprint: Adam state: 2× param size (m and v).

parts.name: Self-distillation RL (slime)
parts.role: Z.ai's 'slime' RL pipeline: SFT bootstrapping, then iterative self-distillation rollouts on agentic / reasoning / coding benchmarks. Uses GRPO-family losses.
parts.algorithm:
for round in range(R):
    rollouts = sample(policy, prompts)
    rewards  = score(rollouts, judge)
    loss     = grpo_loss(rollouts, rewards)
    policy.update(loss)

parts.name: Parallelism Plan: TP × EP × PP × DP
parts.role: Multi-axis parallelism plan. Typical training config: TP=8 (within node), EP=8 (groups for the 160 experts), PP=8 (DualPipe/1F1B for 96 layers), DP=N replicas. The group-limited router caps EP all-to-all traffic.
parts.algorithm:
rank = (dp, pp, ep, tp)
# attention/FFN GEMMs sharded by TP within node
# experts assigned to EP groups across nodes
# group-limit: token can land on at most 4 of 8 EP groups
# 96 layers split into PP stages via DualPipe / 1F1B
improvement_ideas: ZeRO-1 over DP for optimiser-state sharding.

parts.name: 23 T-token Corpus (3-stage curriculum)
parts.role: Pre-training data plate: ~23T tokens in 3 stages — general → reasoning-heavy → long-context (32K → 128K via YaRN).
